notes: Function names resolved from evaluated (not literal source) text -- e.g. a register holding a function name. jmush's parser normally resolves names statically at parse time (MushcodeParser); DynamicFunctionExpression is only built when the accumulated name-candidate depends on a substitution. Two documented divergences are deliberately NOT in this corpus (see DynamicFunctionExpression's/RegisterFunctions' javadoc): unknownfunc(add(1,2),3) stays fully literal on the real oracle (contaminated-accumulator effect jmush doesn't replicate), and setq(a,text) succeeds silently on real TinyMUSH's letter-named extended registers, which jmush doesn't model.
